channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DFA Chief Highlights Connection Between North Sea and South China Sea Security
item.description: During her visit to The Hague, Netherlands, Department of Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro emphasized the strategic link between North Sea security and the stability of the South China Sea...

item.title: Dubare Elephant Camp Temporarily Closed to Tourists Pending Safety Guidelines
item.description: Following the unfortunate death of a tourist from Tamil Nadu, who was caught in a violent altercation between elephants Kanjan and Marthanda, the Dubare Elephant Camp near Kushalnagar in Kodagu has be...

item.title: Agnico Eagle Announces $2.4B Redevelopment of Hope Bay Gold Mine in Nunavut
item.description: Agnico Eagle Mines Limited, the world's second-largest gold producer, will begin redevelopment of the Hope Bay Mine in Nunavut, Canada, according to Natural Resources Canada. The company acquired the ...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns Grounded in SA as Moroccan Authorities Withhold Landing Clearance
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns’ preparations for the CAF Champions League final second leg against AS FAR have been seriously disrupted after their chartered plane failed to depart South Africa. The team, includin...

item.title: Vincent consults Detective Andres regarding his investigation on Walter in 'The Alibi' Episode 45
item.description: In the latest episode of the popular Filipino TV series 'The Alibi,' Episode 45, Vincent seeks the guidance of Detective Andres as he continues his personal investigation into Walter. The episode delv...

item.title: Delhi Court Rejects Umar Khalid’s Request for Interim Bail in 2020 Riots Case
item.description: On Tuesday, a Delhi court refused to grant interim bail to activist Umar Khalid in connection with the 2020 Delhi riots conspiracy case. Khalid had sought 15 days of bail to attend the Chehlum ritual ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Highlights Pakistan’s Role as Regional Stabiliser During Address at Quetta Staff College
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has stated that Pakistan is playing the role of a net regional stabiliser and is contributing positively to peace and stability not only in the region but also beyond. Wh...

item.title: Discovery of a Deep-Sea Limpet at Nearly 6 Kilometers Below the Pacific Surface
item.description: In the summer of 2025, scientists from the Japan Agency for Marine-Earth Science and Technology (JAMSTEC) conducted a deep-sea expedition 500 kilometers southeast of Tokyo using the crewed submersible...

item.title: April Inflation Increases to 2.8% Driven by Rising Gas Prices, StatsCan Reports
item.description: Statistics Canada has reported that the annual inflation rate in April rose to 2.8%, primarily influenced by higher gasoline prices. The increase marks a continuation of upward pressure on consumer pr...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Investigation Into Apartheid-Era TRC Cases
item.description: The Khampepe Commission of Inquiry is currently underway in Newtown, Johannesburg, focusing on the investigation and prosecution of perpetrators from the apartheid era. This commission has been set up...

item.title: Gowon Urges Politicians to Avoid Do-or-Die Politics and Promote Fair Elections
item.description: Former Nigerian Head of State, General Yakubu Gowon (retd.), don urge all politicians for the country make dem stop all the do-or-die politics. He talk this one during the launch of him autobiography ...

item.title: Key Updates: Falta Repoll Withdrawal, SC Upholds Stray Dog Orders, Rupee Hits Record Low
item.description: In West Bengal’s Falta constituency, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) candidate, Jahangir Khan, has announced his withdrawal from the upcoming repoll scheduled for May 21, citing the constituency’s develo...

item.title: Tanzania Hosts TIMUN Conference to Highlight Youth Leadership in Global Development
item.description: Tanzania recently hosted the TIMUN Conference, where leaders strongly insisted on the central role of young people in driving positive changes across the world. The event brought together various part...

item.title: Church Leaders Encourage Prayer and Legal Action Amid Political Turmoil
item.description: Church leaders in the Philippines are urging the public to respond thoughtfully to the current political tensions in the country. Cebu Archbishop Alberts S. Uy emphasized the need for honesty, transpa...

item.title: UK Government Proposes Voluntary Cap on Key Grocery Prices Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The UK government is reportedly urging supermarkets to voluntarily limit price increases on essential groceries such as eggs, bread, and milk. In return, retailers could benefit from relaxed packaging...

item.title: DCI Issues Strong Warning to Protest Organisers Amid Ongoing Nationwide Fuel Strike in Kenya
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has issued a stern warning to individuals organising, financing or participating in any criminal acts during the ongoing nationwide fuel protests in Ke...

item.title: Tanzania invests in advanced science and technology skills for Vision 2050
item.description: For long time, Tanzania struggle with lack of highly skilled scientists and tech experts, which slowed development plans. Now, government is taking big steps through programs like Samia Scholarship Ex...

item.title: PDP under Turaki waives screening requirement for Jonathan ahead of 2027 primaries
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), under the leadership of Kabiru Turaki, don announce say dem go waive all screening requirements for former President Goodluck Jonathan as e dey prepare for the part...

item.title: The King and Queen visit Belfast with enhanced security measures
item.description: The King and Queen have arrived in Belfast, greeted by a significant security presence. Their visit is part of an official engagement, reflecting ongoing diplomatic and cultural ties. Authorities impl...

item.title: April Inflation Rises to 2.8% Driven by Higher Gas Prices
item.description: Statistics Canada reports that consumer inflation in Canada increased to 2.8 per cent in April 2026, up from 2.4 per cent in March compared to the same period last year. The primary factor behind this...
